picks.narrative: IBP makes WSH 57.6% fair versus a 48.5% no-vig market price — a 8.6pp gap. The model isn't leaning on a marquee matchup here — no single baseball factor rises to a headline; the disagreement is with the posted price itself. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +104 still sits inside the playable range (to -116). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.

picks.narrative: Model and market disagree on TEX by 7.4pp — 63.0% fair against a 54.8% price. The largest lift is pitcher quality (xfip) (~+4.9pp): MacKenzie Gore 3.87 xFIP vs Cristian Javier 9.35 xFIP. Home field adds another ~+1.0pp. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -125 still sits inside the playable range (to -144). The win condition is five-plus quality innings from MacKenzie Gore and avoiding early bullpen exposure.

picks.narrative: IBP makes ARI 37.8% fair versus a 33.3% no-vig market price — a 5.0pp gap. No individual baseball factor clears the bar for a headline here — this is not a single visible mismatch, it is the model pricing the side differently than the market. The main pushback is home field (~-1.1pp): LAD's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +205 still sits inside the playable range (to +196). The read here is the price, not the profile — the model just makes this a better side than the posted number does.

picks.narrative: The model prices PHI at 51.2% to win where the no-vig market implies 46.1%, a 4.9pp disagreement. The largest lift is lineup quality (~+0.6pp): PHI projects above its season baseline against this pitcher handedness. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +116 still sits inside the playable range (to +112). The edge is the model pricing PHI differently than the market rather than one of the marquee starter/lineup/bullpen buckets — worth taking at the number, not chasing past it.
